The Sibis are identified by some with the &ivas of the Rgveda,
which is not convincing. There is a Sibijataka written by Arya-
sura. These Jatakas know of two Sibi settlements with their capi-
tals at Aristapura and at Jetuttara, Jattaraur of Alberuni, modern
Nagari, 11 miles north of Chitor. Fa-hian locates the scene of the
Sibijataka at Shorkot in the Punjab. Thus it cannot be said with
certainty who and where exactly these Sibis were in the south. It is
possible that some branches of them might have migrated to the
south.
Gonarda is said to he the birth place of Patanjali. It is also
mentioned in the Markandeya Purana. In the SuttanipSta it has
been placed between Ujjayiniand Vtdi^a. However, Patafljali was
not a south-Indian.
The Rsikas are spoken of along with Vidarbha, Mahi§aka,
Asmaka and Anupa, according to the Ramayana and the Mahabha-
rata.
